_comment: Adversarial-review panel composition, as data. The driver (run-review.ps1) reads this to know which reviewers to run, with which wrapper, model, and reasoning effort. Swap or add reviewers HERE, not in code. INVARIANT: the active (enabled) reviewer set must span at least `minVendors` distinct vendors -- uncorrelated error across vendors is the entire point of the panel. v2: all vendors are subscription-backed CLIs (see `wrapper`); the metered-API wrappers are retained as `fallbackWrapper` for when a subscription is unavailable. See docs/METHODOLOGY-v2.md.

wrappers._comment: Each wrapper is a sibling .ps1 with a uniform contract: -Instruction/-InstructionPath -DiffPath [-FindingsPath] [-ContextPath] -Model [-Effort] [-RepoPath] [-OutPath] [-UsageSidecarPath], returning the review text on stdout and a non-zero exit on failure. Subscription-backed CLI wrappers (claude/codex/kimi/gemini) are primary; api wrappers (openai) are fallbacks. `effort` and `repoAccess` are honoured only where the underlying CLI supports them; the driver introspects each wrapper and omits a flag it does not declare.

reviewers._comment: Anthropic reviewer #1 (Sonnet). Subscription-backed via Claude Code (Agent tool in-process) or `claude -p` (claude-review.ps1) on other hosts. Repo-aware and hard read-only (--permission-mode plan). Reviewer != judge: the Phase-3 judge is Opus, so a Sonnet reviewer keeps the anthropic axis decorrelated from the adjudicator.
reviewers.label: Claude Sonnet

reviewers._comment: Anthropic reviewer #2 (Fable 5). Same vendor as B -- a correlated, not independent, second vote, handled by vendor-weighted consensus (Phase-3 brief) and a merged Observatory row. Fable is now available in perpetuity on the Claude subscription. Canonical telemetry id: claude-fable-5.
reviewers.label: Claude Fable 5

reviewers._comment: OpenAI reviewer. PRIMARY = Codex CLI (codex-review.ps1), subscription-backed via a ChatGPT Pro login -- no metered credits. FALLBACK = openai-review.ps1 (direct Chat Completions API) for when no subscription is available; the driver falls back on primary non-zero exit and flags the run degraded-to-API. Repo-aware and HARD read-only (codex exec --sandbox read-only). Note: codex model ids differ from the API model id below; the codex wrapper uses the subscription's default model unless the id is codex-native, and records `model` for telemetry grouping only.
reviewers.label: GPT (Codex)

reviewers._comment: Moonshot reviewer. Subscription-backed via the Kimi Code CLI (kimi-review.ps1), Allegretto OAuth login -- no API fallback (sub-only). Model is kimi-code/kimi-for-coding (K2.7 Coding, Standard) -- the CLI's own default_model. NOTE: kimi-for-coding-highspeed bills ~3x the credits of Standard for equivalent review output (the highspeed multiplier, not extra work), so a modest panel run burned ~30% of the weekly allowance; Standard is the credit-sane default. Do NOT confuse Standard with kimi-code/k3 -- K3 is a distinct 1M-context model (freshly launched, capacity-congested as of mid-Jul 2026), not the Standard tier of K2.7. Swap to kimi-code/k3 here only if a chunk genuinely needs the 1M window; swap to kimi-code/kimi-for-coding-highspeed only if speed is worth the 3x credit burn. repoAccess is FALSE by default deliberately: Kimi Code has no per-invocation read-only flag and its global default_permission_mode is typically 'yolo' (auto-approve writes/bash), so the wrapper runs it hermetically (throwaway scratch cwd + copied context, repo NOT in the workspace). It is still a strong 4th vendor blind+context. To enable repo-aware call-path tracing, set repoAccess=true -- the wrapper then adds the repo via --add-dir, guarded by a read-only prompt + the git working tree (accidental writes are detectable/revertable), NOT a hard sandbox. Canonical telemetry id: kimi-for-coding.
reviewers.label: Kimi (standard)

reviewers._comment: Google reviewer (Gemini). Subscription-backed via the Gemini CLI on the user's Google One entitlement. Diff-only (repo-blind): pass the key repo files via -ContextPath. The wrapper pins gemini-2.5-pro.
reviewers.label: Gemini

alternates._comment: Opus is reserved as judge (Phase 3) and verifier (Phase 4) -- not a blind reviewer. Do NOT enable as a reviewer: reviewer==judge collapses the anthropic axis to the adjudicator's model seen twice.
alternates.label: Claude Opus

roles._comment: Judgment roles the host runs after the driver's blind+cross-examine spine. Phase 3 (adjudicate) is a single Opus judge for one coherent voice. Phase 3.5 (judge-audit, opt-in) is a cross-vendor sanity check on the adjudicated report -- default a non-Anthropic vendor. Phase 4 (verify) draws from a CROSS-VENDOR pool (Sonnet/Kimi/Codex), rotated by vendor, so verification is not an Anthropic monoculture.
